title: Sarcocadetia funiformis
intro: Sarcocadetia funiformis, also known as the West African Dog Apple, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unique, yellow or red fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Sarcocadetia funiformis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are commonly used in West African folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
